programme.title: It's Sew Easy
programme.desc: Your home reflects who you are. Using an easy smocking technique, sewing enthusiast Cheryl Sleboda makes colorful, patterned pillows to showcase her style. Blogger Heather Valentine combines unusual materials to create a handy caddy for her studio. With hand-dyed wool and nubby fibers, author Lisa Shepard Stewart stitches a lush wall-hanging.

programme.title: It's Sew Easy
programme.desc: The key to making a signature piece work is accessories. Dress it up or down with a perfect scarf or wrap. Cookie Gaynor starts with a few simple tricks and fabric choices for scarves to increase your wardrobe quickly and stylishly. Lesson 2 of the Little Black Dress features silhouettes and sewing on silk with a French seam, all with Angela Wolf. Joanne Banko gives plain pashmina wraps a makeover with bobbin work from both the embroidery and sewing side.

programme.title: Off Our Needles
programme.desc: Take 10 minutes to celebrate Mom! The ladies chat knitting for Mom, gifts for Mom and even take a call from, yup, you guessed it, their mom.

programme.title: Hands On Crafts for Kids
programme.desc: Let's get creative with inspiration from the forest, sea and sky. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs and Katie Hacker make a friendly hedgehog then Katie creates a wiggly fish. Franz Spohn scratches off paint to make colorful animal images and Jenny makes a gelatin print Valentine's Day card.

programme.title: Man About Cake

programme.title: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
programme.desc: George Vondriska shows you how to make an occasional table to provide a convenient place for drinks, TV remotes, or a decorative lamp. He demonstrates the unique process of ebonizing, an alternative to stain used to give wood a rich, dark finish. With attractive tapered legs and a ceramic tabletop, this table is a great project to advance your woodworking skills.

programme.title: Fresh Quilting
programme.desc: Mix fabrics, colors and prints to make modern statement. Krista Fleckenstein makes mixed media appliquÃ© with die-cut shapes of wool, leather, felt and linen. Modern quilter Riane Menardi shares ideas for promoting your guild through local media. Christina Cameli mixes rulers with free motion stitches to accurately stitch a variety of motifs.

programme.title: The Midnight Quilt Show
programme.desc: Ready to find your center? Tonight, Angela becomes one with the piecing as she makes a stress-relieving half-square triangle quilt.

programme.title: The Midnight Quilt Show
programme.desc: On the season finale of Season 6 of the Midnight Quilt Show, follow along as Angela shares her clever take on a braided quilt pattern she created herself!

programme.title: It's Sew Easy
programme.desc: We expand our view of classics by adding surface embellishments to personalize your garment. Cindy Hogan makes appliques from embroidery to add detail to any garment. Then, it's our next lesson in the Little Black Dress from Angela Wolf and different seam finishes including the Hong Kong. Last is embroidery expert Eileen Roche and easy dress embroidery for beautiful style.

programme.title: It's Sew Easy
programme.desc: Step off the traditional path and sew your own signature style. Designer Kay Whitt adds decorative hem bands to create colorful on-trend fashions. Pattern designer and custom clothier Angela Wolf shows how to create a perfect hem. Then, she redesigns vintage ready-to-wear to channel today's runway look.

programme.title: Beads, Baubles & Jewels
programme.desc: Jump-start your creative ideas. Krystal Wick and Cynthia Kimura blend beads, stones and pearls in endless combinations to make bracelets, necklaces and earrings. In her Beading Lesson, Katie uses knotted cords and beads to create great bracelets in minutes.

programme.title: Beads, Baubles & Jewels
programme.desc: Author Cindy Pankopf shares her technique for using antique salt spoons and other found objects as molds to create beautiful clay jewelry. Learn how to use silver metal clay to create unique rings using those same molds. Katie creates a bracelet from knotted wire and beads in her Beading Lesson.

programme.title: Scrapbook Soup
programme.desc: It's fun to take and share selfie photos. Amy Tan gets selfies out of the camera and onto artful pages. Ashley Horton uses fancy frames to capture a bit of herself in her designs.Amy Tan has a tip for adding stitches to patterned paper. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer creates a fun photo series from every day activities.

programme.title: Make It Artsy
programme.desc: Get out your rose-colored glasses, or whatever props you need to take a fresh look at your art. Host Julie Fei Fan Balzer starts the show with a faux shibori dyeing demonstration. Then, our tool guy, Joe Rotella creates unique frames using small strips of wood for a rustic or metallic look. Finally, Tori Weyers returns this season for more artistic inspiration. Every other week she has a lesson on a new way of looking at an aspect of art through your sketchbook. Today it's all about words. Explore interesting textures and shapes through writing and letters.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life
programme.sub-title: Vinyasa for Life: From Cobra to Camel
programme.desc: This vinyasa class applies what you learned in Day 1 so you can play with classic backbends and steady transitions. Schuyler guides you through Cobra (Bhujangasana), Locust (Salabhasana), and Camel (Ustrasana), finishing off with Bridge (Setu Bandhasana) and spine-clearing twists. Take it slow and build strength!

programme.title: Gut Health with Dr. Mary Pardee
programme.sub-title: Day 3: IBS and other Gastrointestinal Issues
programme.desc: Today, we dive into the most commonly diagnosed functional GI disorder, Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS)

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life
programme.sub-title: Vinyasa for Life: Foundations of Forward Bending
programme.desc: In this class, you begin with a brief anatomy of forward folds, followed by a dive into Standing Forward Fold (Uttanasana) and Wide-Legged Forward Fold (Prasarita Padottanasana). Regardless of your flexibility, this lab is designed to help you to safely find length through the entire back chain of your body.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life
programme.sub-title: Vinyasa for Life: Forward Bending for Forward Thinking
programme.desc: Use what you learned in Day 4 to stretch the hamstrings and your backbody safely and effectively. Bring attention to your spine in Downward Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana) and Intense Side Stretch (Parsvottanasana), before working on Hand-to-Big-Toe Pose (Padangusthasana) with a strap. Finish in a supported backbend to counter your diligent forward folding.

programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency
programme.sub-title: Section 1: The Addiction Frequency & The Power of Breath
programme.desc: Breathing is the single most important thing we do. Addiction is characterized by a sense of lack, of missing something. And when you breathe poorly, like when anxious or angry, you’re caught up in your head and aren’t breathing well and that sends a signal to your body that perpetuates the addiction frequency. Deep, long breathing is how we interrupt this pattern.

programme.title: Ecstatic Breathwork
programme.desc: Everyone has difficult emotions, but we don’t have to turn these feelings into a big production.

programme.title: Yoga For All
programme.desc: Your Day 1 practice offers an invitation to slow down and take stock.

programme.title: Yoga For All
programme.desc: Your Day 2 session invites you to use the tools of the at-home yoga practice to befriend your body and your breath. Build on the art of noticing from Day 1 as we expand our vocabulary with a focus on a strong foundation. This session encourages a healthy flow of energy up and down the spine and lovingly guides you to learn how to take responsibility for your experience on the mat.

programme.title: Qigong: The Eight Brocades
programme.desc: Qigong is considered one of the five branches of Chinese Medicine, a foundational basis for martial arts, and a long-standing spiritual practice revered in Daoist and Zen traditions. In this video, your qigong teacher, Mimi Kuo-Deemer, introduces you to the etymology of qigong and the history of the 8 Silk Brocades you will practice in this course.

programme.title: Qigong: The Eight Brocades
programme.desc: Let any burdens you carry drop away while you move through this gentle, intentional qigong practice.

programme.title: Balance Your Hormones with Dr. Jolene Brighten
programme.desc: Your hormones work like a symphony. When every instrument is in tune, they play harmoniously. However, if one instrument is out of tune, or one hormone is out of balance, the entire piece is thrown off. Together, we'll decode your symptoms and work with your body to create hormonal balance. This means clear skin, easier periods, less hot flashes, better sex, and more restful sleep. Yes, it can be that good when your hormones are in check.

However, if one instrument is out of tune, or one hormone is out of balance, the entire piece is thrown off. Together, we'll decode your symptoms and work with your body to create hormonal balance. This means clear skin, easier periods, less hot flashes, better sex, and more restful sleep. Yes, it can be that good when your hormones are in check.

programme.title: Balance Your Hormones with Dr. Jolene Brighten
programme.sub-title: Balance Your Hormones - Day 2: Adrenal Glands
programme.desc: This sentence sums it up: The adrenal glands are the foundation of hormone balancing. If your adrenals aren't functioning at full capacity, you'll feel it. In today's lesson, we take a deep dive into the hormones that our adrenal glands produce. We'll start to get an idea of why taking care of and keeping these glands happy is so important.

programme.title: Balance Your Hormones with Dr. Jolene Brighten
programme.sub-title: Balance Your Hormones - Day 3: The Thyroid
programme.desc: How common are thyroid conditions? Women are 5 to 8 times more likely than a man to develop thyroid disease. If your thyroid isn't functioning optimally, every single cell in your body will feel it, including your ovaries. A thyroid function that is too high or too low can lead to symptoms that include low heart rate, fatigue, weight loss, and menstrual irregularities.

A thyroid function that is too high or too low can lead to symptoms that include low heart rate, fatigue, weight loss, and menstrual irregularities.

programme.title: Balance Your Hormones with Dr. Jolene Brighten
programme.desc: When it comes to having a calm, chilled-out mindset or the sweet feelings of love, you can thank progesterone. On the flip side, suboptimal levels of this hormone can cause you to have headaches, breast tenderness, inability to fall asleep, and painful or heavy periods. Together, we'll learn what you can do to get your body producing the right amount of progesterone. Because this hormone is everything when it comes to feeling at peace.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life
programme.sub-title: Vinyasa for Life: From Cobra to Camel
programme.desc: This vinyasa class applies what you learned in Day 1 so you can play with classic backbends and steady transitions. Schuyler guides you through Cobra (Bhujangasana), Locust (Salabhasana), and Camel (Ustrasana), finishing off with Bridge (Setu Bandhasana) and spine-clearing twists. Take it slow and build strength!

programme.title: Qigong: The Eight Brocades
programme.desc: This practice begins by tending to both the Yin and Yang meridians in your body. You’ll start with a soothing self-massage, which is beneficial to the health of your vagus nerve, especially if you experience overwhelm or often feel depleted. Allow yourself to feel calm and bright as you practice the fifth and sixth Silk Brocades.

programme.title: Hacking Your Healthcare
programme.sub-title: Day 1: Health Care vs. Sick Care
programme.desc: Today, we talk about a revolutionary approach to health called Functional Medicine, and how you can have a healthy doctor-patient relationship.

programme.title: Hacking Your Healthcare
programme.desc: Today we start with a discussion of reference ranges, and how what medicine considers as “normal” may not be optimal for your health

programme.title: Hacking Your Healthcare
programme.desc: How do you know if you're one of the 90% that's lacking in something essential? Let’s talk about the tests you can use to find out, and more importantly, what to do about it.

programme.title: Good Mood Food with Jason Wrobel
programme.desc: For Day 1 of the course, Jason discusses how nutrients in food can directly affect your neurons and neurotransmitter function. He also shares why gut health is absolutely crucial for brain health and how to make super creamy and delicious dairy-free Coconut Yogurt.

programme.title: Good Mood Food with Jason Wrobel
programme.desc: It’s time for a little science class, but way more fun and interesting! Jason shares the basics of neuroscience: what a neurotransmitter is and how it functions, along with the top three emotional neurotransmitters that are key to your mood and overall emotional health.

programme.title: Deep Relaxation with Tracee Stanley
programme.desc: This Deep Relaxation session revolves around cultivating sensitivity towards the energetic body, with the aim of cleansing and rejuvenation.

programme.title: Hacking Your Healthcare
programme.sub-title: Day 1: Health Care vs. Sick Care
programme.desc: Today, we talk about a revolutionary approach to health called Functional Medicine, and how you can have a healthy doctor-patient relationship.

programme.title: Hacking Your Healthcare
programme.desc: Today we start with a discussion of reference ranges, and how what medicine considers as “normal” may not be optimal for your health

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson
programme.sub-title: Relationships: Relationships and Your Spiritual Journey
programme.desc: Today's lesson is a bit of a reality check, as we'll begin to question the current way we look at relationships, romantic or not. How we see ourselves and our relationship to God influences our view of our relationships with other people. Today we're going to examine that view from a place of love.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson
programme.sub-title: Relationships: How to Have Miraculous Relationships
programme.desc: A miracle happens when you stop thinking with fear and start thinking with love. Your greatest power lies in your mind. To establish stronger connections, you need to go into every situation requesting to be an instrument of love. When you approach your interactions with love, you'll begin to have more miraculous relationships.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson
programme.desc: In the following lesson, we'll discover how the ego mind influences both our way of thinking and our relationships. We'll also identify the unconscious barriers we've built that cause us to form these judgments and thoughts. That's where the problem is, and that's where the solution lies.

programme.title: Balance Your Hormones with Dr. Jolene Brighten
programme.desc: Your hormones work like a symphony. When every instrument is in tune, they play harmoniously. However, if one instrument is out of tune, or one hormone is out of balance, the entire piece is thrown off. Together, we'll decode your symptoms and work with your body to create hormonal balance. This means clear skin, easier periods, less hot flashes, better sex, and more restful sleep. Yes, it can be that good when your hormones are in check.

programme.title: Balance Your Hormones with Dr. Jolene Brighten
programme.sub-title: Balance Your Hormones - Day 2: Adrenal Glands
programme.desc: This sentence sums it up: The adrenal glands are the foundation of hormone balancing. If your adrenals aren't functioning at full capacity, you'll feel it. In today's lesson, we take a deep dive into the hormones that our adrenal glands produce. We'll start to get an idea of why taking care of and keeping these glands happy is so important.

programme.title: Balance Your Hormones with Dr. Jolene Brighten
programme.sub-title: Balance Your Hormones - Day 3: The Thyroid
programme.desc: How common are thyroid conditions? Women are 5 to 8 times more likely than a man to develop thyroid disease. If your thyroid isn't functioning optimally, every single cell in your body will feel it, including your ovaries. A thyroid function that is too high or too low can lead to symptoms that include low heart rate, fatigue, weight loss, and menstrual irregularities.

programme.title: Balance Your Hormones with Dr. Jolene Brighten
programme.sub-title: Balance Your Hormones - Day 3: The Thyroid
programme.desc: How common are thyroid conditions? Women are 5 to 8 times more likely than a man to develop thyroid disease. If your thyroid isn't functioning optimally, every single cell in your body will feel it, including your ovaries. A thyroid function that is too high or too low can lead to symptoms that include low heart rate, fatigue, weight loss, and menstrual irregularities.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson
programme.sub-title: Relationships: Relationships and Your Spiritual Journey
programme.desc: Today's lesson is a bit of a reality check, as we'll begin to question the current way we look at relationships, romantic or not. How we see ourselves and our relationship to God influences our view of our relationships with other people. Today we're going to examine that view from a place of love.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson
programme.sub-title: Relationships: How to Have Miraculous Relationships
programme.desc: A miracle happens when you stop thinking with fear and start thinking with love. Your greatest power lies in your mind. To establish stronger connections, you need to go into every situation requesting to be an instrument of love. When you approach your interactions with love, you'll begin to have more miraculous relationships.

programme.title: Weight Loss Master Class
programme.sub-title: Weight Loss Master Class - This Is Why You 'Can’t Lose Weight'
programme.desc: The media tells you, 'Don’t worry! You’re big and you’re beautiful.' That you are healthy at any size. Nice to hear, but it simply isn’t true. Pharma companies and many in the medical community are now going to tell you it’s genetic — a disease of the brain that results in an insatiable hunger. They do this because surgeries and weight loss drugs are profitable. In this lesson, we will address these issues one by one.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson
programme.desc: In the following lesson, we'll discover how the ego mind influences both our way of thinking and our relationships. We'll also identify the unconscious barriers we've built that cause us to form these judgments and thoughts. That's where the problem is, and that's where the solution lies.

programme.title: Weight Loss Master Class
programme.sub-title: Weight Loss Master Class - Calories: Everything You Need to Know
programme.desc: Let’s use a quick analogy: If your body is a car, then reverse is actively gaining weight, neutral is not gaining or losing, and drive is weight loss… Ultimately you have been eating more than you are burning, which is what put the weight on in the first place. By controlling the calories in, we generally shift the car from reverse to neutral. No longer gaining, but not really losing a ton. By adding exercise into the mix we throw the car into drive without compromising your metabolism or muscle mass.

programme.title: Weight Loss Master Class
programme.sub-title: Weight Loss Master Class - Balancing Hormones for an Optimized Metabolism
programme.desc: This lesson discusses the connection between hormones and metabolism. The goal here is to get your hormones in balance, which will regulate your appetite, optimize your BMR, and help you maintain lean body mass. The good news is that to do this we don’t need a deep dive on each and every hormone because what’s good for one hormone is typically good for all of them. Meaning, the recommendations in this lesson help bring your entire body into balance — so you don’t have to deal with each hormone on a case-by-case basis.

programme.title: Weight Loss Master Class
programme.sub-title: Weight Loss Master Class - The Relevance of Macros & Micros
programme.desc: At long last you have arrived at the diet section of this course. And while technically this episode is about food quality, this is where Jillian answers all your questions about popular fad diets. Keto, Carnivore, Paleo, Pegan, Vegan, Vegetarian, Low Fat, Intermittent Fasting, Low Glycemic. Which is best?! Find out what matters most for weight loss.

programme.title: Weight Loss Master Class
programme.sub-title: Weight Loss Master Class - The Role of Exercise in Weight Loss
programme.desc: Finally, the exercise episode! Exercise is the most powerful lever you can pull when it comes to longevity, health, and weight loss. It’s also the number one form of preventative medicine and helps prevent yo-yo dieting. Here Jillian provides clear guidelines for how much and what types of exercises you should be building into your weekly routine.

programme.title: Weight Loss Master Class
programme.sub-title: Weight Loss Master Class - Why Do We Have Insatiable Hunger?
programme.desc: Hopefully by now you understand that you gain weight because you have eaten more energy than you have burned in your day and stored that energy in your fat cells. But, have you ever wondered WHY you are overeating — i.e. eating more than you are burning? Why do we have such insatiable appetites when our bodies are usually pretty good at staying in balance? And no… it’s not because you have no willpower. This episode digs into where the physiological and psychological hunger is coming from — and how to cope with it.

programme.title: Weight Loss Master Class
programme.sub-title: Weight Loss Master Class - Way Beyond Willpower: Managing Relationships and Environments to Beat Temptation
programme.desc: Willpower is like a muscle: If you use it long enough without a break, it fatigues. It’s the moment at the Mexican restaurant when they go to drop the chips and you say, 'No thanks.' If the chips sit on that table, there is no chance you won’t end up eating the basket. So, let’s go over some basic techniques you need to program into your behavior and environment to safeguard yourself against momentary vulnerabilities. We also cover some serious boundary setting, so get ready.

programme.title: Weight Loss Master Class
programme.sub-title: Weight Loss Master Class - Weight Loss Cheat Codes
programme.desc: There are certainly things that can add a plus to our A, but these aren’t replacements for eating better and moving more. Nothing in this lesson will come close to impacting your results like diet and exercise. Nonetheless, Jillian offers her thoughts on cold exposure, heat exposure, and supplements.

programme.title: Weight Loss Master Class
programme.sub-title: Weight Loss Master Class - Your Complete Weight-Loss Plan
programme.desc: A big goal (if you have one) can feel a bit intimidating or overwhelming. This episode is here to help remove any of those anxious feelings with something called a goal pyramid. This simple technique will help you know exactly what to do and when to make that vision a reality!

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: We have returned to the powerhouse of Portugal for an edge-of-your-seat galactic battle. If all the planets collided at once, it wouldn’t compare to the bang of this show. Get ready for the battle of the beasts; it’s the heaviest show in the universe, the Strongman Champions League.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: SCL UK has landed in Gloucester it is workout war fitness for the ferocious, it’s going to be a a battle of the brutal, a rampage of the roofless. This is going to be a massacre of the muscles. You know it by now, but just in case you don’t, this is strongman champions league.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.sub-title: SCL 2023 Season 9, Stage 10 - World's Strongest Viking
programme.desc: On the menu this week it’s medieval madness, we’ve come back to the middle ages to test our noble men through a muscle gaining of events. They are hoping and we are hoping that one will survive to take the title of the world’s strongest Viking. The official World's Strongest Viking is this time in Finland.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: This week is a good one, can you feel the energy? Sit still, just look at me and try and feel the energy, because I can tell you, the atmosphere here is electric, it’s explosive, there is a pure elemental force going through these athletes' veins. I’m shaking because it’s Poland, it’s powerful, it’s strongman champions league, and you know what I'm going to say, get ready because it’s about to get heavy.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: SCL Poland part 2 - Who will win the title in Poland and take 25 points for the world ranking. It is very exciting on the top, in part 1 between Poland and Latvia. Also an interview with Mateusz Kieloszkowski, the all time favorite champion from Poland.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: It’s time to show our strength and gain respect from the gods of Olympus. Our gladiators have many events set out by no other than Hercules himself. The coliseum is ready here on this mythological island of Cyprus, so we have landed in Limassol were again 12 Top Strongmen are battling for the title and ranking points.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: The only and official world championship Truckpulling for its 10th year, again at the Strongman Champions league. With the 3 times Worldchampion Kelvin de Ruiter – defending his title. This championship has trucks in all shapes and sizes which is a lot like our athletes, there all different pay loads, but does the biggest win or is it the strongest.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: Years and years of determination has brought us here today, when all the walls felt like they were caving in perseverance. When their bodies were screaming, hurting, shouting stop, beneath. These are the things which make up a man, these are the things that bring you world finalists. This episode they fight for the greatest title of all, they fight to put their names in the history books forever.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: Part 2 of the World Finals of the Strongman Champions League tour 2023 at the Sarajevo Centar Skenderija in Bosnia. 3 Events was done already and amazing results have been set. programme.title: World Series of Poker
programme.desc: Nearly 800 rebuys would take this field size to 1,334 and a prize pool of over $1.26 million. The final table would be stacked with tournament veterans led by Daniel Negreanu, John Juanda, Mike Matusow, Lee Markholt, Paul Phillips, and Ram Vaswani as all eyes were on the $365,900 first-place prize.

programme.title: World Series of Poker
programme.desc: The 831-entrant field created a $1.14 million prize pool in the first $1,500 No-Limit Hold'em event of the 2004 WSOP. Poker author Blair Rodman, and tournament regulars Joe Awada, Scott Epstein, and Kent Washington held seats at the final table, along with "The Crew" member Scott Fischman as the $300,000 first-place prize was up for grabs.

programme.title: World Series of Poker
programme.desc: The biggest No-Limit Hold'em buy-in of the 2004 WSOP outside of the Main Event would attract 254 entrants and create a prize pool of $1,193,800. Barry Greenstein and Dewey Tomko fell short of reaching the final table, as WSOP veterans James Sousa and Paul "Eskimo" Clark headlined the final table ahead of Lee Watkinson, Thomas Keller, Martin de Knijff, and Young Phan as they chased the $382,020 first-place prize.

programme.title: World Series of Poker
programme.desc: The $1,000 Ladies Event attracted 201 entrants and would see the final table headlined by Karina Jett and Susie Isaacs as nine lucky ladies look to capture the WSOP bracelet and $58,530 first-place prize.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.sub-title: "Are Turkey Population Numbers Declining? | Turkeys For Tomorrow"
programme.desc: Phillip Culpepper heads westward to Alabama to learn more about Turkeys for Tomorrow (TFT) and its mission. This education organization is all about sharing information and helping to grow the turkey population. TFT promotes America's hunting traditions, conservation, and education through the timeless collaboration of hunters and wildlife professionals to ensure we all have turkeys for tomorrow.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Back at the Raven for bow camp and everything is coming together. Randy has a close encounter with one of the OG bucks on the property while Nate closes the deal on a big five year old.

programme.title: Ultimate Warrior Challenge - Episode 06
programme.desc: Ultimate Warrior Challenge is one of Europe's leading MMA shows, since it's inception in 2006 it's has grown steadily and delivered some of the most exciting MMA fights Europe has seen, with a truly Internationalfight roster, UWC has built a solid reputation for bringing some of the Worlds toughest fighters to square off in the UWC octagon, fighters from the UK, USA, Brazil, Sweden, Italy, Africa, Poland, France, Cyprus, Greece have all fought in the UWC cage. UFC stars such as Simeon Thoreson, Luke 'Bigslow' Barnatt, RiccoRodriguez,Thierry "The African Assassin" Sokoudjou, Phil De Fries, Paul Kelly to name but a few, have graced the UWC cage. UWC continues to grow and to bring the fight fans across Europe the fights they want to see.

programme.desc: Ultimate Warrior Challenge is one of Europe's leading MMA shows, since it's inception in 2006 it's has grown steadily and delivered some of the most exciting MMA fights Europe has seen, with a truly Internationalfight roster, UWC has built a solid reputation for bringing some of the Worlds toughest fighters to square off in the UWC octagon, fighters from the UK, USA, Brazil, Sweden, Italy, Africa, Poland, France, Cyprus, Greece have all fought in the UWC cage. UFC stars such as Simeon Thoreson, Luke 'Bigslow' Barnatt, RiccoRodriguez,Thierry "The African Assassin" Sokoudjou, Phil De Fries, Paul Kelly to name but a few, have graced the UWC cage. UWC continues to grow and to bring the fight fans across Europe the fights they want to see.

programme.title: The Great Siberian Traverse
programme.desc: The Great Siberian Traverse documents a 6,000-mile ski journey through Russia, along the Trans-Siberian Railway. The documentary, created by Sherpas Cinema in collaboration with Powder Magazine and The North Face, showcases a fringe backcountry skiing community, deep Siberian powder, and skiing�s ancient origins. Team skiers Ingrid Backstrom, Callum Pettit, and Nick Martini share their insights along the adventure.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Not even the arena floor is safe in this week’s eliminators, as the competitors go all out to best the opposition. One robot manages to take control in the heat finals.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: An impressive weapon manages to fight off two robots simultaneously, ripping through armor and insides. As one of the finalists is still reeling from the previous rounds, will they manage to come back and take the finals?

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Heat winners gather for the beginning of The Fourth Wars semi-finals. Pinball Warrior and Sumo Bash make way for the brutal knockout competition.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: The last stage before the Grand Finals, and the pressure is on. With this level of carnage not all robots may leave the arena as they fight for the title, and their lives.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: The Fourth Wars is coming to an end, beginning with two rounds of playoffs, where four will be whittled down to two. Of the remaining two, one will become Robot Wars: The Fourth Wars champions.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: The Extreme series begins with a vengeance battle to settle a bitter rivalry. Two All-stars battles to put the fan favourites up against each other and a final battle with the Challenge belt at stake to up the pressure for the competitors.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: This action-packed episode features several battles, including Wild Card Warriors, Middleweight Championship and Vengeance. All building up to the main event; Mayhem Battle, where three veterans fight for glory.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: The battles this episode include; All-stars, another Mayhem battle and the Challenge belt. Robots from the UK, USA, The Netherlands and Germany fight for your entertainment and their pride in International Inferno.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: A rivalry will be settled in tonight’s main event, a vengeance battle between Razer and Pussycat. Razer claims in their last fight Pussycat had broken a “Gentleman’s Agreement” by causing unnecessary damage after the robot was immobilised.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Tag Team Terror, Mayhem and Vengeance all precede this episode’s main event, a one-off Armed Forces Mayhem. Robots from the British Army, Navy and Royal Air Force go to war to prove who is most efficient in combat.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Two robots fight over the title “Best showman” in this episode’s Vengeance Main Event. In the build-up, we see a three way International Melee between the UK, Belgium and The Netherlands, Tag Team Terror, Challenge Belt and Mayhem battle.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Six robots will be reduced down to one in this annihilator episode. Qualifiers from the Mayhem battles previously in the series. Which competitor will battle through the four rounds and defeat the opposition in the final.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: The Extreme series returns to its normal format after the first Annihilator. Four mixed battles, followed by a Main Event, which in this episode is an All-Stars battle between Pussycat and Stinger.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Shunt and Dead Metal prepare to be let loose from the sidelines in this episodes Main Event, House Robot Rebellion. Three unfortunate competitors will be put into the arena as the House Robots have free reign in this destructive episode.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: We have reached the Quarter Finals for the All-Stars battles in this episode’s Main Event and another Mayhem battle puts another three robots up against each other.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Four of the best robots armed with rear-hinged flippers go against each other to prove whose best in Flipper Frenzy. Other battles on the card are the Challenge Belt, Tag Team Terror, Wild Card Warriors and Mayhem battle.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Current World Champions Razer are matched against fierce competitor Firestorm in this All-stars match. Four robots make their way to the arena to take part in the German Melee, with the winner securing a place in the Second World Championship.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: The Featherweight Championship takes place in this episode, along with the Challenge Belt, Mayhem battle and more Wildcard Warriors. All these build-up to the Main event, the Tag Team Terror finals.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: The second annihilator was supposed to be between the six winners of the mayhem qualifiers, but due to the severity of damage from the battle, two of the robots had to be replaced. Will the two replacements seize this opportunity?

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: The UK are up against renowned German Engineering in a UK vs Germany battle. Two more rivals have a chance to settle the score in another vengeance battle and other unmissable battles take place before the long awaited All-Stars Grand Final.

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Learn more about this infamous Robot and the family that built it. With an unforgettable design and devastating weapon, this competitor became an instant icon.

programme.title: Robot Wars

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Using competitors own video diaries, exclusive action from the pits and the best of the battles, Philippa Forrester will get down to the nuts and bolts of Robot Wars, The Second Wars.

programme.title: Robot Wars

programme.title: Robot Wars

programme.title: Robot Wars

programme.title: Robot Wars

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Italy is one of the very few nations in the world that regularly gets three major extreme natural phenomena – volcanos, flooding, and avalanches. Eastern flanks of the volcano to slip by up to two metres, and many houses on the flanks of the volcano experienced structural damage. The eruption also completely destroyed the Rifugio Sapienza, on the southern flank of the volcano. The Rifugio was the site of a cable car station which had previously been destroyed in the 1983 eruption; both have now been rebuilt. The most recent eruption was in November 2006.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.sub-title: Angry Planet | By Camel to Timbuktu
programme.desc: It is time for “Angry Planet” to do a serious desert episode. What better place to do it than this fabled city in the middle of the mighty Sahara Desert.This almost mythical place really does exist. And it really is still possible to travel there by camel caravan, riding the infamous “Ships of the Desert”. Extreme heat, dehydration, possible sand storms, frigid night temperatures and desert predators are our potential companions for the trip.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.sub-title: Angry Planet | Java - Land of Fire
programme.desc: Geoorge explores Java at the heart of the Ring of Fire.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Possibly the home of the deadly Marberg Ebola virus, Kitum Cave is high on Mt.Elgon straddling the Kenya-Uganda border. The deep, waterfall-shrouded cave has been carved out over millennia by elephants in search of salt. When host george kourounis gets bitten by one of the bats…well, you’ll have to see the show to find out what happens.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Costa Rica is a tiny country jammed with diversity and adventure. George explores the volcanoes of Costa Rica with an international team of scientists, rafts down whitewater rivers in full flood, canyoneers down raging waterfalls, and handles some of deadliest snakes this tropical country has to offer.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: George links up with two experienced polar adventurers to explore the frigid Canadian arctic in the depths of winter. The team first uses skis to cross the fractured sea ice of Frobisher Bay, then dog teams to head north through the spectacular Auyuittuq Mountains.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: George and three side-kicks head straight into the “Bearcage”, looking to get beat up by the biggest, baddest hailstones that Texas and Oklahoma can throw at them. They find it – and their stormchasing vehicles don’t look too pretty after coming through these massive storms.

programme.desc: Only 60 or so miles up the Hudson River from Manhattan, the Hudson Valley is a luscious land rich with history. Tony explores the area in search of the usual -- good food, good drink and good people.

programme.title: Lucky in Love Ep 1
programme.desc: Canadians Sophie and Anna go on a journey of discovery, luck and love in the Cradle of Liberty, Boston, Massachusetts.

programme.title: Lucky in Love Ep 2
programme.desc: Gabby and Jono try their luck in panoramic Salt Lake City, Utah.

programme.title: Lucky in Love Ep 3
programme.desc: Percy and Michelle go on a game of chance in the Emerald city of Seattle.

programme.title: United Dates of America
programme.desc: Ruairi, Hardeep, and Luke head south to meet local singles in young and vibrant Charlotte, North Carolina.

programme.title: United Dates of America
programme.desc: Ruairi, Hardeep, and Luke try their luck dating in artsy Providence, Rhode Island.

programme.title: Lucky in Love
programme.desc: Danny and Mara try their luck in the Flip Side of Florida, Jacksonville.

programme.title: Lucky in Love
programme.desc: Alana and Josh try their luck in Fort Worth, Texas, where cowboys and cosmopolitans walk hand in hand.

programme.title: Lucky in Love
programme.desc: Rachel and Paul go on a journey of discovery, luck and love through the Big Easy.

programme.title: National Parks Top Ten
programme.desc: Thrill seekers get in on the action at these heart-pumping attractions across America, from an icy trek across Wrangell-St. Elias National park in Alaska to volcano sledding at Hawaii's Volcanoes National Park.

programme.title: National Parks Top Ten
programme.desc: Must-see attractions at America's most beautiful locations are highlighted, from a one-kind-of-a-kind hotel outside of Arches National Park to a therapeutic hot springs in Yosemite National Park.

programme.title: National Parks Top Ten
programme.desc: The best hidden gems in and around America's most beautiful parks are highlighted, from sandboarding at the Great Sand Dunes National Park and Preserve to zip-lining in a cave near Mammoth Cave National Park.

programme.title: National Parks Top Ten
programme.desc: Secret havens and tucked-away retreats at America's most beautiful locations are highlighted, from an oasis in the middle of Death Valley to a skywalk 4,000 feet above the canyon floor near Grand Canyon National Park.

programme.title: Venturing Out: St. Croix
programme.desc: Adventurer and filmmaker Creighton is joined by co-host and local Olivia who challenges him to step out of his comfort zones in surprising ways.

programme.title: Venturing Out: St. Croix
programme.desc: Captain Johnny Carson joins Creighton for a high-seas adventure, leading them on a wild chase through the brush and bramble of St. Croix's famous Buck Island.

programme.title: Facing Waves
programme.desc: Luke Hopkins and Anne Pagano explore one of the world’s great water trails by stand up paddle board - Florida’s Great Calusa Blueway Paddling Trail.

programme.title: Facing Waves
programme.desc: Luke Hopkins and Alex Kellar explore the rugged Oregon Coast by sea kayak, SUP and whitewater kayak.

programme.title: Facing Waves
programme.desc: Ken Whiting explores the beautiful rivers of North Carolina by sea kayak and inflatable kayak.

programme.title: Facing Waves
programme.sub-title: Paddling Creeks and Coves in the Southeast
programme.desc: Ken Whiting tackles whitewater in North Carolina before heading to the renowned Guntersville Lake in Alabama.

programme.title: USA Through the Great Outdoors
programme.desc: Place is inextricably linked to who we are as human beings. See the American Great Outdoors in its purest and most authentic light – through the eyes of locals.

programme.title: Samantha Browns Places to Love
programme.sub-title: Samantha Browns Places To Love: Newport
programme.desc: Welcome to a legendary Ocean destination. With over 300 years of history and miles of coastal beauty, Newport, Rhode Island was once the vacation hotspot for the who’s who of the Gilded Age. Today, it’s still a vacation favorite with stunning architecture, rich history, and an incredible gastronomy scene. Join Samantha as she meets the people past and present who make Newport a Place to Love.

programme.title: Trails & Trailblazers
programme.desc: Join pilot Ariel Tweto as she flies to unexpected places along the trails and wild spaces of America, meeting trailblazers in science, history, and exploration along the way.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Portland, OR, Guy Fieri's checks out a Guamanian food truck serving righteous shrimp fritters and chicken salad. In Wheat Ridge, CO, a family-run comfort food joint's crankin' out killer cannoli and kraut burgers.

programme.title: USA Through Food
programme.desc: A journey through the USA, through the diverse food scenes of six vibrant cities.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain: No Reservations
programme.sub-title: Anthony Bourdain: No Reservations New Orleans
programme.desc: New Orleans, a city known for its love of food, music and life, was forever changed by the storm of Hurricane Katrina; yet today, New Orleans is still a vibrant and spectacular town, and who better to explore the true impact of Katrina than Tony Bourdain?

programme.title: Paul & Nick's Big American Food Trip
programme.desc: The chefs head to the city of Knoxville, East Tennessee to meet and cook for descendants of the first Ulster-Scots settlers in the area – the Ramseys and the Whites. They hit the Market Square Farmers’ Market in downtown Knoxville and visit a 6th generation farmer to find some great beef and they take a trip to a dairy farm to taste some local buttermilk…

programme.title: Paul & Nick's Big American Food Trip
programme.desc: Chefs Paul Rankin and Nick Nairn are in Lexington, Virginia to cook for descendants of the Ulster-Scots Lewis family who settled in the area in the 18th century. Along the way they visit a heritage pig farmer, taste some sparkling cider and they discover some delicious goat’s cheese in the heart of Virginia’s Piedmont region where Paul has a close encounter with a friendly goat…

programme.title: Paul & Nick's Big American Food Trip
programme.desc: Paul and Nick cook for the descendants of the 18th century Scots-speaking poet Robert Dinsmore, and try to source some wild turkey. Will the turkey be a step too far for Brad Dinsmore and his family?

programme.title: United States of Sports

programme.title: Toulouse-Lautrec
programme.desc: The celebrated French artist creates some of the most popular works of the late 1800s.

programme.title: Beethoven - Symphony No. 8
programme.desc: Philippe Jordan and the Orchestre de Paris recorded all of Beethoven's Symphonies.

programme.title: Works for Lute by J. S. Bach
programme.sub-title: Works for Lute by J. S. Bach
programme.desc: French lutenist Thomas Dunford performs various works for lute by J. S. Bach in this intimate chamber music concert, recorded at Concertgebouw Brugge, Belgium, in 2017.

programme.title: Beethoven: String Quartets Nos. 3, 8 & 11
programme.desc: Renowned French string quartet Quatuor Ébène marked the 250th birth anniversary of Ludwig van Beethoven (1770-1827) with a remarkable project: recording all of the great composer's sixteen string quartets.

programme.title: Martha Argerich â€" A Birthday Celebration # 2
programme.sub-title: Martha Argerich â€" A Birthday Celebration # 2
programme.desc: On the occasion of her 80th birthday, Argentinian pianist Martha Argerich explored repertoire for piano and violin in this wonderful concert, recorded at Chateau de Chantilly, France.

programme.title: Works by Mendelssohn & Hensel
programme.desc: A 2020 performance Mendelssohn and Hensel at the Mendelssohn House in Leipzig, Germany.

programme.title: Beethoven: Symphony No. 3 in E-flat major, Op. 55
programme.sub-title: Beethoven: Symphony No. 3 in E-flat major, Op. 55
programme.desc: Maestro Herbert Blomstedt makes his debut with the Lucerne Festival Orchestra at 2020's Lucerne Festival conducting Ludwig van Beethoven's celebrated Symphony No 3 in E-flat major, Op 55, Eroica.

programme.title: Bach - Italian Concerto & Chaconne (arr. Busoni)
programme.desc: At one of Italy's oldest extant Renaissance theatres, the Teatro Olimpico in the northern Italian town of Vicenza, Costanza Principe performs Johann Sebastian Bach's "Concerto italiano" and "Ciaccona," as transcribed for piano by Ferruccio Busoni.

programme.title: Schubert - Symphony No. 8 in B Minor, D. 759
programme.sub-title: Schubert - Symphony No. 8 in B Minor, D. 759
programme.desc: In this performance, Les Dissonances perform Franz Schubert's Symphony No. 8 in B minor, D. 759, also known as the "Unfinished Symphony"; this performance was recorded at Cité de la musique in Paris, France, in 2013.

programme.title: Grieg's Piano Concerto & Beethoven Symphony No. 7
programme.sub-title: Grieg's Piano Concerto & Beethoven Symphony No. 7
programme.desc: Conductor Jan Latham-Koenig leads the Flanders Symphony Orchestra in a concert recorded at Concertgebouw Brugge, Belgium; Felix Mendelssohn's concert overture Die Hebriden, Op. 26; Edvard Grieg's Piano Concerto in A minor, Op. 16.

programme.title: String Quartets Nos. 1, 9 & 10
programme.desc: Quatuor Ébène marked the 250th birth anniversary of Beethoven with a remarkable project:.

programme.title: Fischer conducts Prokofiev and Stravinsky
programme.desc: Iván Fischer conducts the Budapest Festival Orchestra in a concert recorded at the Béla Bartók National Concert Hall in Budapest, Hungary in 2015; the concert opens with Sergei Prokofiev's Overture on Hebrew Themes.

programme.title: Beethoven Violin Concerto & Bizet Symphony in C
programme.sub-title: Beethoven Violin Concerto & Bizet Symphony in C
programme.desc: In this concert, recorded at Opéra de Tours in February 2021, young Venezuelan conductor Glass Marcano leads the Orchestre Symphonique Région Centre-Val de Loire.

programme.title: Mikhail Pletnev Conducts Rimski-Korsakov
programme.desc: Russian conductor and pianist Mikhail Pletnev leads the Russian National Orchestra in performances of Nikolai Rimsky-Korsakov's music.

programme.title: La Bayadere
programme.desc: The Paris Opera Ballet performs the work choreographed by Rudolf Nureyev.

programme.title: Vocal Works by Monteverdi, Frescobaldi a. o.
programme.sub-title: Vocal Works by Monteverdi, Frescobaldi a. o.
programme.desc: Covering four centuries of music: from Girolamo Frescobaldi and Antonio Vivaldi.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Coming upon the lava-fueled dwarven forge, the party begins to craft anti-magical emerald-imbued weapons. That is, until the whole building begins to shake...

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Iron Stone is saved! As thanks, the party receives a bounty of magical crystals and a powerful new iron suit for Arky. Now they head for Rock Bottom, a duegar dwarf city beneath a waterfall in the dark Underground.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Reaching Pelion Darrus, the Party finds it under devastating attack from above and below. One hero makes an unexpected journey, dragging the party into a surprising decision in this weeks episode!

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The Audio Dungeon crew begin their adventure in the world of Andaria. Finndelar (Tom) the wizard, and Hogar (Dan) the ranger, have just finished their two month journey, and have reached their destination; The Sunken City. Finn's mission is to retrieve a magical device created in part by a local craftsman: Arkie Fint. Hogar, an ally of Finn, was hired to guide and protect him. When they arrive, they find themselves in the midst of a large celebration, as well as a mystery regarding their mission...

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Finndelar (Tom) and Hogar (Dan) have arrived in the Sunken City and are now searching for the magical device they have journeyed so far for. After meeting the craftsman Arkie Fint, they travel to Smithy's tavern in hope of finding a clue to their mystery...

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Smithy's party has proven to be entertaining and everyone is enjoying themselves. Meanwhile, during the festivities, Bludgy, the city's most notorious thief is seen sneaking upstairs, drawing the attention of others. Finn and Hogar's mission hangs in the balance...

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Finn and Hogar are tied up below Smithy's tavern along with a few others. Bludgy is about to sell off the crystal rod for a life changing amount ofgold, while Arkie is "retrieving" something important from his workshop.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The party, now in possession of the CRYSTAL ROD, has fled from the thieves in the Sunken City through the underground tunnels. Once outside, they discover the Queen's forces are invading from the sea, bombarding the city. They quickly slip out beyond the walls, north of the city... but to go where?

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The party has been caught trying to escape and with them, the Artifact. Eventually they wake up shackled to a cart, and surrounded by guards on their way back into the occupied Sunken City… at the mercy of the Queen's Forces.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: "The party has adjusted to their new lives the best ways they know, how but they are eager to escape. - - Follow the party as they continue working their 9 to 5 jobs with only one lunch break and four 15 minute coffee breaks fun by the evil Queen’s forces."

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: "The Party is in action, and so is the party. Our would be heroes are posing as entertainment to get aboard the rooks ship and steal back the rod before they can escape. - - Will they succeed?"

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: "Our Party, aka Blackwater Bandits, have earned their name stealing back the rod from the Rook. Now its time for one more performance... the disappearing act. - - Will they succeed in this mid-season finale?"

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The Players awake with no memories and must quickly work together to navigate a dangerous dungeon. The extra twist? They have no idea they are playing their actual characters!

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The party has passed the trial! With acceptance into the city of Anwar, their new goal is recovering those stolen memories...

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Tragedy has struck the party, a member has fallen. Adding to their strife, they now find themselves in yet another tough situation below Anwar.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Hurtling down the water passageway on the journey to The Dark, the party's boat takes a beating. Luckily for them, this means their captor The Lovely Lute takes a beating as well. Now they must try to continue their mission for the Crystal Rod.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.sub-title: The Short Adventures of Thistle & Arky
programme.desc: Who better to go poking around in goblin tunnels than the only members of the party who will fit inside?! But what Arky and Thistle find in the warrens is not what they expected…

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The party finds the dwarven city of Iron Stone. Unfortunately, it is under siege by strange magical creatures. And what has become of that dangerous assassin, the Lovely Lute?

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Coming upon the lava-fueled dwarven forge, the party begins to craft anti-magical emerald-imbued weapons. That is, until the whole building begins to shake...

programme.title: All Access 2nd Drop "BMX Best Trick"
programme.desc: ØAll Access 2nd DropÆ is taking you behind-the-scenes for a never before seen look at all aspects of the 2017 Nitro World Games. Some fresh international faces come through the ranks at open qualifying to go head-to-head with the worldís best BMX riders. Scooter rider Ryan Williams looks to repeat as champion in this event, which isnít even his main discipline.

programme.title: All Access 2nd Drop "FMX Best Trick"
programme.desc: ØAll Access 2nd DropÆ is taking you behind-the-scenes for a never before seen look at all aspects of the 2017 Nitro World Games. Travis Pastrana and an elite group of FMX Pros prepare and practice for the biggest and most dangerous event at Nitro World Games and in the sport of FMX, FMX Best Trick.

programme.title: All Access 2nd Drop "48 Hours"
programme.desc: ØAll Access 2nd DropÆ is taking you behind-the-scenes for a never before seen look at all aspects of the 2017 Nitro World Games. On this episode we experience all the madness, drama, and excitement that occurs 48 hours before the Games leading into the live TV broadcast on NBC.

programme.title: All Access 2nd Drop "Pastrana's Top 10"
programme.desc: The creative mastermind behind the Nitro World Games, Travis Pastrana, breaks down his Top 10 favorite moments from the Games. Travis shares his personal insights about his favorite athletes and moments along with the most inspired and innovative tricks from the 2017 Nitro World Games in Salt Lake City, Utah.

programme.title: Action Figures 1 Bonus Content
programme.desc: Travis Pastrana & his friends have gone mental. Don’t watch this unless you put on a helmet first...Seriously

programme.title: Back to Racing: Northern Nightmare 3 & Crandon World Cup
programme.desc: Travis Pastrana races in both the Northern Nightmare and Crandon World Cup

programme.title: Back to Racing: Sno Drift
programme.desc: Travis Pastrana drifts through icy roads

programme.title: Action Figures 1
programme.desc: Travis Pastrana and friends perform the world's most outrageous and challenging stunts in this feature film

programme.title: The Biggest Week in Action Sports
programme.desc: Biggest Week in Action Sports tells the story of two monumental world first attempts - the FMX Triple Backflip and the BMX Quadruple Backflip - that took place in one extraordinary week at Pastranaland.ò

programme.title: Back to Racing: Freedom 500
programme.desc: Travis Pastrana drives a Crown Victoria in the Freedom 500

programme.title: Nitro Circus Live: We Finally made It
programme.desc: Four riders end up in the hospital as Travis Pastrana and the Nitro Crew head to Sweden to kick off their European tour.

programme.title: Nitro Circus Live: Berlin & Dusseldforf
programme.desc: Travis Pastrana and the Nitro Crew face their toughest challenge yet, 3 shows in 3 nights in front of sold out crowds in Germany.

programme.title: Nitro Circus Live: Just A Couple of Touristis
programme.desc: Midway through their European tour, Travis Pastrana and the Nitro Crew go sightseeing in Prague, hit the go kart track, and pull off their best show in history for a sold-out crowd in Belgium.

programme.title: Nitro Circus Live: The Big One
programme.desc: Travis Pastrana and the Nitro Crew head to Holland to perform in front of a massive crowd of 27,000, their largest show ever. But first, they hold an eating competition that leaves one of them bleeding.

programme.title: Nitro Circus Live: London Calling
programme.desc: Travis Pastrana and the Nitro crew head to London for a tension-filled show with the future of the tour at stake. But the injury bug strikes once again, leaving producers to scramble mid-show to rewrite the second half.

programme.title: Nitro Circus Live: The End of the Road
programme.desc: As Travis Pastrana and the Nitro Crew wrap up their first ever European tour with 2 shows in England, the tour's most inspirational performer suffers a serious injury, leaving unsung heroes to step up in his absence.

programme.title: Nitro Circus Live: Epic Fails
programme.desc: Countdown of the top 10 "epic fails".

programme.title: Tommy the Little Dragon
programme.desc: Tommy is a soft toy who lives with an ordinary panda family. Panda parents have no idea about him, while their cubs cannot live a day without Tommy! With the help of his magic soap bubbles, Tommy teleports kids to wonderful universes full of adventures, to help them discover a vast and captivating world, fight their fears and inhibitions, and learn important family values.

programme.title: Bat Pat
programme.desc: The series follows the comedy adventures of Bat Pat and the Silver brothers Martin, Leo and Rebecca as they unravel mysteries and cross paths with supernatural creatures of the night that lurk, haunt, shriek and dig themselves up in every dark corner of their home in Fogville, a small town that seems to be the center of all things weird in the world, a veritable magnet to ghosts, mummies, witches, creatures, werewolves and things that go bump in the night. Bat Pat will prove that the supernatural isn’t always evil but just misunderstood. Our heroes will come to understand something important: the supernatural “bad guys” are not really bad, but normal people whose actions are misunderstood by the more inhabitants of Fogville and what they really want is... just help!

programme.title: Ollie! The Boy Who Became What He Ate
programme.desc: Ollie is a 6-year-old with a massive imagination, often lost in his own world - flipping, sliding, climbing and rocket-blasting through the house, practicing at being the master of all things. But even masters need to stop and refuel. When meals or snack time interrupt Ollie’s play, he finds himself confronted with the real challenge of childhood; trying new food. To escape his predicament, Ollie uses his magic spoon to transport himself, along with his sister Poppy, and her stuffy Nummy, to a food planet filled with adventure and the very food Ollie stuck his spoon into.

programme.title: Pirate school
programme.desc: On a small remote island surrounded by endless seas, a special Pirate School was opened, where children from pirate families study. Among them are Keel, Fuse and Bobo. The pirate origin does not relieve them from study and normal school duties.

programme.title: The Secrets of Honey Hills
programme.desc: Honey Hills is a small seaside town where all kind of animals live. It's a good neighbourhood. However, mysterious and inexplicable events sometimes cause misunderstandings among neighbours. Then, genius detective Sophie the Owl together with her young assistant Chirp the Squirrel take up the investigation.

programme.title: Pirate school
programme.desc: On a small remote island surrounded by endless seas, a special Pirate School was opened, where children from pirate families study. Among them are Keel, Fuse and Bobo. The pirate origin does not relieve them from study and normal school duties.

programme.title: The Secrets of Honey Hills
programme.desc: Honey Hills is a small seaside town where all kind of animals live. It's a good neighbourhood. However, mysterious and inexplicable events sometimes cause misunderstandings among neighbours. Then, genius detective Sophie the Owl together with her young assistant Chirp the Squirrel take up the investigation.

programme.title: Princess Play Time
programme.desc: The proud Princess likes to do things her own way, but her antics are always joined by her restless brother, Prince, who tends to turn everything topsy-turvy. Any playtime in their room transforms into a fantastic journey through unusual worlds: the children arrange a picnic on the Moon, conduct a detective investigation in a toy city, or search for pirate treasures on islands! Although initially, the Princess and Prince seem to clash and compete, by the end, they always find joy in each other's company and that of their cherished dog, Bona.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: It's countryside chaos as Shaun The Sheep returns to the small screen with his very own series. Peace in the valley turns to mayhem in the meadow as Shaun leads his flock into all sorts of scrapes and scraps, running rings around sheepdog Bitzer when the Farmer's back is turned.

programme.title: Cutie Cubies
programme.desc: An animation series about the adventures of four aliens - Cubies: Captain, Boatswain, Navigator, and Doctor. Having arrived on our planet and disguised as representatives of the local flora and fauna of the cubic form: a yellow chicken, a red rabbit, a blue aquarium and a bush of green grass, they are learning the diverse phenomena of our planet Earth.

programme.title: Monsuno
programme.desc: The series revolves around re-awakened Monster DNA, called Monsuno, that finds its way into the unsuspecting hands of adventure-seeking teenagers Chase, Jinja, and Bren. Accompanied by their Monsuno, Lock, Charger, and Quickforce, they are searching for Chase's father Jeredy Suno, the creator of the Monsuno

programme.title: Heroes of Envell
programme.desc: Present days. Saint Petersburg. Four friends – Art, Kira, Phil and Vic are the coolest gamers in school. Eventually they find a suspicious laptop with «Heroes of Envell» game installed on it. Turns out that this very laptop is a portal to another dimension, a game realm which is full of adventures and real-life danger. The game realm is a blend of medieval, steampunk and futurism. You never know what really awaits our heroes. Just like the design of the game, the game logic is very unpredictable. You start with a quest and then it becomes a race right until it all grows into a tricky brainteaser. But be sure about one thing – there is no boring or easy way in here. The game realm is a blend of medieval, steampunk and futurism. You never know what really awaits our heroes. Just like the design of the game, the game logic is very unpredictable. You start with a quest and then it becomes a race right until it all grows into a tricky brainteaser. But be sure about one thing – there is no boring or easy way in here.

programme.title: Grojband
programme.desc: The story about 12 year old Corey that emotionally torments his sister so that she will write the ultimate lyrics for his garage band.

programme.title: Paper Tales
programme.desc: Animation series “Paper Tales” narrate to children audience in fascinating way amusing adventures of paper animals – a moose named Aristotle and a woodpecker named Knock-Knock – in paper land where everything is made only from paper. Merry adventures come together with entertaining insets “for crafty hands” when the audience learns how to produce all cartoon characters using paper, cardboard, scissors and glue. The project is supported with the site where all flat patterns are provided with the advices how to manufacture the paper world items.This is the way spectator gets involved into a peculiar game that brings out creative work and development of many useful practical skills, distracting kids from permanent attachment to a computer. The “Paper Tales” project is a fascinating and helpful alternative to computer-oriented pastime. The whole cartoon is accompanied by off-screen narrator’s text and exuberant flow of gags. Each episode is planned to last for 5 minutes (a version without insets – 4 minutes). Technique of performance is 3D computer graphics imitating paper application work.

programme.title: Woodventures
programme.desc: "Woodventures" is an animated series for preschool children. The main characters are wooden toys: Kitty Meow, Doggy Woof-Woof, Piggy Oink, Horsey Yee-Ho-Ho and Elephant Pawoo. The toys live in a small wooden town (the prototype of a child's play room). Every toy has its own unique personality and reacts to different circumstances appropriately. With the help of the invisible narrator, the toys, along with the audience solve different tasks, play and take part in adventures. "Woodventures" is a musical series with lots of songs and dances. Some of the songs represent our characters and appear in each series, and some of the songs are related to the events in the series, so it is only found in that particular episode.Each episode presents a playful and learning environment.

programme.title: Gummy Bear and Friends
programme.desc: Gummy lives in an apartment with his friend Harry the Chameleon, while his other friend Kala the Cat hangs out in a ninja dojo on the roof. Together they go on wild and wacky adventures in their building and hometown. Enjoy the fun and crazy adventures!

programme.title: The Adventures of Peter and Wolf
programme.desc: A sitcom about the adventures of ordinary schoolboy Peter and talking Wolf connecting the world of people to parallel universes, where there live robots and vampires, werewolves and zombies, giants and aliens, fairies and centaurs, clones and invisible men! The characters will have to solve locals' problems and regularly save the world.

programme.title: Magic Lantern
programme.desc: Animation series “Magic Lantern” tells little viewers wonderful adventure stories, based on classic literature plots. Little video games fan Mike and his smart sister Kate watch and discuss each story presented as a strip film. Funny stories will help seeing classic stories from the new side and prove that world classic literature is exciting and interesting.

programme.title: Eliot Kid
programme.desc: When a tooth falling out turns into a hunt for the “giant mouse”, when the city’s sewers transform into Indiana Jones’ white rapids or when a trip to the toilets becomes an expedition to a haunted house, well then you’ve entered the wonderful world of Eliot Kid!

programme.title: Back to Back
programme.desc: Embark on an electrifying journey as they boldly leap from one heart-pounding expedition to the next, fueled by their unbreakable bond and insatiable thirst for excitement. Get ready to be swept away in a whirlwind of adrenaline-pumping quests, where danger and discovery await them at every turn.

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends
programme.desc: Things get even more epic in Season 3 as Talking Tom and Friends try new ways to make their dreams come true, making more friends (and enemies) along the way!

programme.title: I'll Get You! Holiday
programme.desc: The action takes place in all year round resort, where Wolf doesn't chase Hare to eat him anymore: now it's all about sports and competition. However, in his crazy chase after recognition and victories the grey hooligan often uses methods that aren't really fair, which provokes awkward and funny situations.

programme.title: Everybody Loves a Moose
programme.desc: An oddball buddy series between an over ambitious boy, Jack and his pal Moose, a melodramatic self-confessed expert in everything. Except he isn't! Despite his best intentions, he always messes up and although Jack may not get what he want, you can't help being sucked in by Moose's relentless optimism and zest for life!

programme.title: Fishtronaut
programme.desc: The series tells the story of Fishtronaut, a fish in a spacesuit, similar to reverse scuba gear, which allows him to fly and breathe out of water.

programme.title: Pincode
programme.desc: Grown up Kikoriki characters rock the world with their scientific superpowers and tell the kids scientific facts in a most entertaining way. The crew follows Captain Pin as they learn about the world we live in and share this knowledge with us.

programme.title: Robocar POLI
programme.desc: The courageous and swift police car POLI, the powerful fire engine ROY, the intelligent AMBER, and the flying HELLY are all here to save the day!

programme.title: The Most Famous Mystery We'll NEVER Solve
programme.desc: Nearly 200 years ago, a mysterious teen boy arrived in Nuremberg. The event ignited the Kaspar Hauser story: a tale of weird history, an unsolved mystery that endures today.

programme.title: The $68 Million Instant Movie Disaster (Polavision)
programme.desc: Nearly 50 years ago, the Polavision camera blended Polaroid’s revolutionary instant film with on-demand home video – and the result was a landmark advance in analog technology.

programme.desc: Discover the fascinating prototype behind our iconic lightsaber from Star Wars.

programme.title: Hacksmith
programme.desc: Take a closer look at some of Star Wars' most iconic equipment, from the chilling carbon freeze to Han Solo's blaster and the indestructible Mandalorian armor—unveiling the tech behind the legends!

programme.title: After Antarctica
programme.desc: After Antarctica follows polar explorer Will Steger’s journey as an eyewitness to the greatest changes to the polar regions of our planet.

programme.title: What If: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Venture to Earth's limits as we explore the planet's most extreme environments and the life that thrives there!

programme.title: The $2,000 Video Phone From 1987
programme.desc: It's been 150 years since we started thinking about and criticizing live video communication. We still haven't fully answered the technology's most basic question: does anyone really want to be seen?

programme.title: How to Survive
